channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris is heading to North Carolina to survey Helene's aftermath one day after Trump visited
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris is heading to North Carolina on Saturday as the state recovers from Hurricane Helene, arriving there one day after a visit by Republican Donald Trump, who is spreading false claims about the federal response to the disaster.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina Sens. Tills, Budd call for military leader to oversee Hurricane Helene response
item.description: Two US senators are calling for a military leader to lead the response to Hurricane Helene amid criticism of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden says he and Kamala Harris are 'singing from the same song sheet' — as she tries distancing herself
item.description: In a surprise press briefing appearance on Friday afternoon, President Joe Biden made concerted efforts to link Kamala Harris to the work of the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Michigan, Harris doesn't get hoped-for firefighters endorsement amid shifting labor loyalties
item.description: REDFORD TOWNSHIP, Mich. (AP) — It was the perfect place to welcome the endorsement of the firefighters union — a gleaming new firehouse in a blue-collar town just outside of Detroit in the key battleground state of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Civil rights groups ask to extend voter registration deadlines in hurricane-ravaged states
item.description: Democrats and civil rights groups are asking election officials in the states ravaged by Hurricane Helene to give voters more time to register for the Nov. 5 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ayanna Alexander, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Retired New Jersey State Police trooper who stormed Capitol is sentenced to probation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A retired New Jersey State Police trooper who stormed the U.S. Capitol with a mob of Donald Trump supporters was sentenced to probation instead of prison on Friday, as the federal courts reached a milestone in the punishment of Capitol rioters.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Assassination attempts and new threats have reshaped how Donald Trump campaigns
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump was onstage at a rally on Long Island last month, talking about taxes, when he appeared momentarily spooked by something he'd spotted over his shoulder. “I thought this was a wise guy coming up,” he explained, joking that he was getting his elbow ready to fight back.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Kemp make rare joint appearance in Helene aftermath
item.description: Former President Trump and Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp (R) on Friday made their first public appearance together in years, putting aside simmering tensions to highlight how Hurricane Helene has devastated the state.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court declines to block Biden limits on methane, toxic pollution
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday declined to block Biden administration limits on planet-warming methane from oil and gas production and toxic pollution from coal plants.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Some Republicans embrace ‘pro-choice’ label, infuriating Democrats
item.description: A few Republicans running for competitive or Democratic-leaning congressional seats are adopting and reviving a label that was nearly extinct in their party: pro-choice.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court will weigh Mexico's $10 billion lawsuit against U.S. gun makers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court said Friday it will decide whether to block a $10 billion lawsuit Mexico filed against leading U.S. gun manufacturers over allegations their commercial practices have helped caused much bloodshed there.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Yorkers think Eric Adams should resign after corruption indictment: poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Nearly 70% of New York City residents think Mayor Eric Adams should resign following his corruption indictment, according to a Marist College poll released Friday. Another 63% of New Yorkers think Gov. Kathy Hochul should remove Adams from his position if he does not resign.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump calls Liz Cheney a 'low IQ war hawk' after appearance in support of Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump called former Republican Rep. Liz Cheney a "low IQ war hawk" after her endorsement of Vice President Kamala Harris on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Programming alert: NewsNation to feature PA Senate race tonight
item.description: NewsNation will have live coverage of the Pennsylvania senate debate between Bob Casey and David McCormick.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Politically charged rumors and conspiracy theories about Helene flourish on X
item.description: False claims about the hurricane and the government’s response got millions of views on social media. Emergency management researchers say it makes it harder for useful information to surface.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: In excerpt from new memoir, Melania Trump says women have the ‘right to choose’ abortion
item.description: The former first lady says she is a longtime supporter of abortion rights. Her memoir is coming out a year after former President Donald Trump said he was "able to kill Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Early voting begins in downtown Chicago
item.description: Early voting for the November election begins Thursday in downtown Chicago, with DuPage County seeing nearly double the amount of voters who turned out early in 2020, and early voting in all 50 wards opens up next Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: Are Nevada voters swayed by promises of 'no tax on tips'?
item.description: Reporter John Langeler from KLAS in Las Vegas joins "The Hill" to discuss whether Nevada voters are swayed by proposals to eliminate federal taxes on tips.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump in new memoir calls abortion a 'woman's fundamental right': Report
item.description: “Why should anyone other than the woman herself have the power to determine what she does with her own body?" asks the former first lady.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Full Debate: Kaine, Cao audition to represent Virginia in US Senate
item.description: The first was Wednesday's meeting between Virginia Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine and Republican challenger Hung Cao on Norfolk-based WAVY-TV.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Meet the candidates running for San Diego City Attorney
item.description: There are only two candidates in the running to become San Diego City Attorney after incumbent Mara Elliott ends her last term in office in December.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutors lay out new evidence in Trump election case, accuse him of having 'resorted to crimes'
item.description: A court filing unsealed Wednesday argues that the former president is not entitled to immunity from prosecution over his failed bid to remain in power.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DHS warns of risks around Oct. 7, November election in annual threat assessment
item.description: “We are, of course, aware that that anniversary may add even more fuel to an already challenging and heightened threat environment,” a DHS official said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Vance-Walz VP debate
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance (R) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) met face-to-face in New York City on Tuesday in the first and likely only vice presidential debate of the election cycle, a high-stakes moment that comes a little more than a month before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: State of Democra-Z: Takeaways from the vice presidential debate
item.description: The vice presidential debate is not expected to push many voters to one side or another, but it offered a closer look at the men who could be second in command. We check in with two Gen-Z voters about what stood out to them.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris voiced her support Wednesday for striking port workers while bashing her rival former President Donald Trump after tens of thousands of longshoremen at ports along the East Coast and Gulf of Mexico walked off the job.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Vance, Walz address school shootings
item.description: Walz said the solution is more research into gun violence. Vance said we have to increase security in our schools.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Walz, Vance double down on dueling abortion views
item.description: November 2024 will mark the first U.S. presidential election since the Supreme Court overturned its decision in Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee plastics factory employee recounts narrow escape through floodwater
item.description: Impact Plastics employee Zinna Adkins stepped outside her workplace at 10:54 a.m. on Friday and captured water rising in the parking lot on her cellphone. But at that time, she said no supervisor informed employees they were free to leave.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, Fox 5

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Are convicted felons allowed to vote in Illinois?
item.description: With just five weeks until the 2024 presidential election, most U.S. citizens over the age of 18 are eligible to vote as long as they are registered. However, for some individuals, the right to vote may be more complicated due to a criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Israel begins ground offensive in Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military pressed its ground incursion into southern Lebanon on Tuesday, calling the operations “limited incursions” that are targeting Hezbollah militants.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin, Kat Lonsdorf, and Miguel Macias, MPR News

item.title: Vance and Walz are both veterans. Here's what it means to have a vet VP
item.description: For the first time since 1996, both major parties have picked a veteran for vice president — a fact that some veterans' groups are hoping it leads to greater understanding of military and vet issues.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence, MPR News

item.title: Harris and Trump change their campaign plans after Hurricane Helene's destruction
item.description: Hurricane Helene is scrambling the presidential candidates' plans this week. Democratic nominee Kamala Harris is cutting short a campaign visit to Las Vegas to return to Washington for briefings. Republican candidate Donald Trump is heading to Georgia to see the storm's impact.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, JILL COLVIN and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, WFLA, NBC
